interview_style.description: Amazon bar raiser interview methodology. Deep behavioral dives using Leadership Principles, relentless follow-up probing, and technical interviews that test both depth and breadth.
interview_style.tone: Professional but persistent. Friendly surface, sharp underneath. Never lets a vague answer slide. Will ask 'tell me more about that' until they find the edge of your experience.
interview_style.pacing: Structured and time-boxed. Typically 55 minutes: 5 min intro, 40 min questions, 10 min candidate questions. Each behavioral question gets 15-20 minutes of probing.
interview_style.interviewer_persona: Senior security engineer who has done this 200 times. Calm, methodical, takes notes. Occasional warmth but always returns to probing. Uses silence effectively.

behavioral_emphasis.description: Behavioral is half the evaluation at Amazon. Technical skills get you in the room; LPs get you the offer.
bar_raiser_focus: Pushes hard on 'I vs we' in action statements
Probes for specific dates, numbers, team sizes
Asks 'what did YOU do' when candidate says 'we'
Tests for genuine ownership vs taking credit
Looks for learning and growth, not just results
Always asks 'what would you do differently'
Watches for consistency across multiple stories

technical_emphasis.description: Technical interviews focus on applied knowledge and scenario-based thinking. Not trivia. They want to see how you think through problems.
focus_areas: Emphasis on scale: 'how does this work at 100K endpoints?'
Connection of technical decisions to business impact
Preference for candidates who ask clarifying questions
Values depth over breadth: better to go deep on one topic than shallow on five

follow_up_patterns: You said 'we'. What specifically did you do?
What would you do differently if you could do it again?
How did this experience change how you approach similar problems?
Walk me through the exact conversation you had.
What was the alternative you didn't choose, and why?
